page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is equipped with 372.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per virtual core. This configuration establishes a balanced resource allocation profile suitable for workloads requiring substantial parallel processing capabilities without specialized memory or compute ratios.

This server type does not include integrated local storage or bundled GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that the 96 vCPUs deliver consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants on the host hardware.

Given the balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high core count, the c4d-standard-96 is designed for multi-threaded applications, large-scale web serving, enterprise application hosting, and general-purpose backend services. It offers a resource-dense environment for workloads that benefit from high core counts but do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-96 is a dedicated general-purpose server instance from the c4d family, featuring 96 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 372.0 GB of RAM. With a balanced allocation of 3.88 GB of memory per core, this instance is designed for multi-threaded workloads that require high parallel processing capacity. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. Its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance, making it suitable for enterprise applications, large-scale web serving, and general-purpose backend processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-96 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 372 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for balanced multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A high-core general-purpose server featuring ninety-six dedicated virtual processors and balanced memory allocation for parallelized enterprise workloads.
